source_texts: 쌍두취 행진곡 가을 학기가 되자, ○○일보사에서 주최하는 학생계몽운동에 참가하였던 대원들이 돌아왔다. 오늘 저녁은 각처에서 모여든 대원들을 위로하는 다과회가 그 신문사 누상에서 열린것이다.
오륙백 명이나 수용할 수 있는 대강당에는 전 조선의 방방곡곡으로 흩어져서 한여름 동안 땀을 흘려 가며 활동한 남녀 대원들로 빈틈없이 들어찼다.
폭양에 그을은 그들의 시커먼 얼굴! 큰 박덩이만큼씩 한 전등이 드문드문하게 달린 천장에서 내리비치는 불빛이 휘황할수록, 흰 벽을 등지고 앉은 그네들의 얼굴은 더한층 검어 보인다.
만호 장안의 별처럼 깔린 등불이 한눈에 내려다보이도록 사방의 유리창을 활짝 열 어제 쳤건만, 건장한 청년들의 코와 몸에서 풍기는 훈김이 우거진 콩밭 속에를 들어간 것만치 나후 끈 후끈 끼친다.
정각이 되자 P학당의 취주악대는 코넷, 트럼본 같은 번쩍거리는 악기를 들고 연단 앞줄에가 벌려 선다. 지휘자가 손을 내젓는 대로 힘차게 연주하는 것은 유명한 독일 사람의 작곡인 쌍두취 행진곡(雙頭鷲行進曲)이다. 그 활발하고 장쾌한 멜로디는 여러 사람의 심장까지 울리면서 장내의 공기를 진동시킨다.
악대의 연주가 끝난 다음에, 사회자인 이 신문사의 편집국장이 안경을 번득이며 점잖은 걸음걸이로 단 위에 나타났다.
"에― 아직 개학을 아니 헌 학교도 있어서 미처 올라오지 못한 대원이 많을 줄 알었습니다.
그런데 뜻밖에 이처럼 성황을 이루어서 장소가 매우 협착한 까닭에, 여러분끼리 서로간 친하는 기회를 드리려는 다과회가 무슨 강연회처럼 되었습니다."
하고 일장의 인사를 베푼 뒤에 으흠으흠 하고 헛기침을 해서 목소리를 가다듬더니,

"금년에는 여러 가지로 지장이 많았는데도 불구하고 작년보다도 거진 곱절이나 되는 놀라울 만한 성적을 보게 됐습니다. 이것은 오직 동족을 사랑하는 여러분의 열성과, 문맹을 한 사람이라도 더 물리치려는 헌신적 노력의 결과인 것이 물론입니다. 그러므로 주 최자측으로서 여러분의 수고를 감사할 뿐 아니라, 우리 계몽운동의 장래를 위해서 경축하기를 마지않는 바입니다."
처음에는 늦게 들어오는 사람들 때문에 수성수성하던 장내가 인제는 기침 소리 하나 없이 조용해졌다. 사회자는 말을 이어,
system_prompt:
You are a professional document translator.

TASK:
Translate ONLY each SOURCE_TEXT item into English naturally and accurately.
Use ITEM_CONTEXT only to understand local meaning, terminology, tone, abbreviation hints, and nearby document flow. Do not translate or copy ITEM_CONTEXT unless the same content appears in SOURCE_TEXT.

OUTPUT FORMAT:
Return ONLY a valid JSON array in the form [{"id": 0, "t": "translated text"}].
Keep each "id" unchanged.
Put translated text only in key "t".
Do not output reasoning, analysis, markdown fences, prose, or <think> blocks.

Target language guard:
- Output must be in English, except for numbers, symbols, URLs, formulas, file paths, email addresses, and explicitly preserved proper nouns/technical terms.
- Do not leave accidental mixed-language fragments.
- Do not include Korean, Hangul, Hanja, Kanji, Kana, or Chinese-character fragments unless explicitly preserved as proper nouns, identifiers, or technical terms.
- Translate Korean/Hanja source text into English words; do not leave Hanja characters as stylistic glosses or parenthetical remnants.

DOCUMENT TERM MEMORY:
- Use analysis_hint entries only to understand source-side meaning. They are not translation targets.
- Use the preferred target whenever one is provided. The status field (initial_seed, review_required, etc.) is tracking metadata — it does not mean the target is optional or tentative for translation purposes.
- Do not choose among alternative targets. If a preferred target is given, use it. Do not substitute your own judgment unless local context makes the term clearly inapplicable.
- If pre_judge_policy is source_meaning_only or blocked, do not apply any target; use the meaning note only.
- 쌍두취 행진곡 [쌍두취 행진곡 / 雙頭鷲行進曲] | preferred=Double-Headed Eagle March | meaning=Title of a specific musical piece played in the story.

- 계몽운동 [계몽운동 / 학생계몽운동] | preferred=enlightenment movement | meaning=The specific historical/social activity the characters are participating in (educating the masses). | avoid=illumination campaign

- 대원 [대원] | preferred=member | meaning=Members of the enlightenment movement teams. | avoid=brigade / soldier

- 쌍두취 행진곡 (Double-Headed Eagle March) [쌍두취 행진곡 (Double-Headed Eagle March) / 쌍두취 행진곡 / 雙頭鷲行進曲] | meaning=A specific piece of music played by the brass band. The Hanja (雙頭鷲) confirms the meaning 'Double-Headed Eagle'. Likely a reference to a German or Western march popular in that era.

- 계몽운동 (Enlightenment Movement) [계몽운동 (Enlightenment Movement) / 학생계몽운동 / 계몽 대원] | meaning=A specific historical/social activity where educated students go to rural areas to teach reading/writing and raise political/social awareness among peasants.

- 대원 (Daewon) [대원 (Daewon) / 계몽 대원 / 대원들] | meaning=Refers specifically to the 'members' or 'corps members' of the enlightenment movement, not general military personnel. They are students acting as volunteers.

- ○○일보사 [○○일보사] | meaning=Sponsor/Organizer of the Student Enlightenment Movement. | full_form=[Redacted] Daily Newspaper Company | role=Sponsor/Organizer of the Student Enlightenment Movement.

- 여러 사람 [여러 사람]

- 오늘 저녁 [오늘 저녁]

- P학당 [P학당] | meaning=Educational institution providing the brass band. | full_form=P Academy / P School (Specific name redacted or abbreviated with 'P') | role=Educational institution providing the brass band.

- 것이다 [것이다]

- 목소리 [목소리]


DOCUMENT TRANSLATION MEMORY:
- Use this only to maintain continuity with earlier translated scopes.
- Do not copy this memory into the translation.
- This memory is lower priority than Document Term Memory.
- Never infer term translations from this memory.
- Use this memory only for discourse continuity, section flow, register, and style continuity.
- If this memory conflicts with Document Term Memory, follow Document Term Memory.
- Memory mode: external markdown continuity memory with relevant sections selected for this chunk.
- Approximate prompt memory budget: 6000 tokens.
Relevant markdown continuity memory:
# Document Continuity Memory
- Target language: English
- Document format: docx
- Purpose: section flow, discourse continuity, register, and style continuity only.
- Not a glossary: terminology decisions belong to Document Term Memory.


PRE-TRANSLATION DOCUMENT CONTEXT:
- document_type: Literary Fiction (Novel/Short Story)
- domain: Early 20th Century Korean Literature, Social Reform, Rural Enlightenment
- workflow: Narrative progression involving public gatherings, rural fieldwork, and interpersonal dialogue
- product/system: N/A (Fictional narrative)
- purpose: To depict the struggles and interactions of student activists engaged in a literacy and enlightenment movement in rural Korea
- audience: General readers of Korean literature, scholars of colonial-era social movements
- content_structure: Chronological narrative with scenes shifting between formal assembly halls, rural villages, and private domestic settings
- translation style: Descriptive, dialogue-heavy, period-specific vernacular (mix of formal speech and colloquialisms); tone: Earnest, occasionally satirical, empathetic towards the poor, critical of social barriers

- Use this as source-only document context, not as locked terminology.
- Apply the document domain, workflow, product/system context, audience, and style when choosing meanings, terminology, register, and label phrasing for the current SOURCE_TEXT.
- Prefer domain-appropriate translations over generic literal translations when the source term is ambiguous.
- Do not treat this section as a glossary or a final target-term decision.
- Do not add domain knowledge that is not supported by the source text, Document Term Memory, or this pre-translation context.

DOMAIN / WORKFLOW CONTEXT:
- The text depicts a 'Student Enlightenment Movement' (학생계몽운동) where urban students travel to rural areas to teach literacy.
- Key settings include a formal assembly hall (likely in Seoul) for reporting results, and primitive rural villages (farm/fishing communities) where the actual teaching takes place.
- Themes include gender roles (male vs. female activists), class differences (urban students vs. rural poor), and the hardships of colonial-era rural life.
- Specific cultural markers include 'P-hakdang' (P Academy) brass band, traditional food (barley rice, chicken soup), and specific clothing descriptions (chima, jeogori).

PARTICIPANTS AND ROLES:
- 동혁 (Donghyeok): Male protagonist, student activist, likely a university student involved in the movement. | evidence: Frequent mentions (210+), described as having an 'athlete-like cheerful laugh', wearing a school uniform, and giving speeches.

- 영신 (Yeongsin) / 채영신 (Chae Yeongsin): Female protagonist, student activist, attends a women's missionary school ('×× 여자 신학교'). | evidence: Frequent mentions (259+), explicitly named 'Chae Yeongsin' in an introduction speech, described as wearing modest clothing compared to trendy Seoul women.

- 건배 (Geonbae): Rural contact/host, member of a 'Farmers' Association' (농우회), married with a child. | evidence: Mentioned with 'wife' (아낙/아내) and 'child'; described as having a dark, sun-tanned face from farm work.

- 백씨 (Mr. Baek): Mentor figure, experienced speaker/traveler, host of the gathering. | evidence: Referred to as 'Baek Seonsaeng' (Teacher Baek); hosted the evening gathering; traveled to Europe/America.

- S군 (S-gun): Comic relief character, student, skilled guitarist. | evidence: Described as a 'clown' (익살꾼) of C College, plays Hawaiian guitar, mimics songs.

STYLE GUIDANCE:
- The text alternates between formal reported speech (speeches at the assembly) and colloquial, sometimes dialect-heavy dialogue (rural scenes).
- Character names are often followed by particles or verbs that indicate action flow (e.g., '하고 동혁은'); ensure the syntactic link between the dialogue/action and the subject is preserved.
- Descriptions of poverty and rural life are vivid and sensory (smells, textures); maintain this realism without over-sanitizing.
- Gender dynamics are a key subtext; note the distinction in how male and female characters are addressed and described (e.g., Yeongsin's modesty vs. Donghyeok's boldness).


CONTEXT AND ABBREVIATION HINTS:
- Resolve local meaning and ambiguous abbreviations from TABLE_TITLE, SECTION_HEADING, and ABBREVIATION_HINTS when they are provided.
- Applicable abbreviation hints override the model's default expansion.
- Example: if ABBREVIATION_HINTS says "Proc. = Processing", interpret "Proc." as "Processing" throughout the chunk.

Element handling:
- Translate meaning naturally, not word order.
- Preserve IDs, numbering, dates, formulas, URLs, file paths, and source structure.
- Preserve proper nouns/acronyms unless context, terminology, or ABBREVIATION_HINTS resolves them.
- Do not infer a document-wide glossary unless memory/terminology provides it.
- Headings stay concise; bullets stay parallel; table cells, labels, field names, and UI/task labels stay compact; legal/financial/technical prose stays precise.
- Preserve paragraph flow and local meaning.

STYLE OPTIONS:
- purpose: default
- formality: formal_hamnida
- terminology: preserve_key_terms

STYLE GUIDE RULES:
- Preserve the source meaning, information density, and logical relationships. Do not add, omit, or reinterpret content.
- Preserve the document structure and the function of each element. Translate headings as headings, list items as list items, and table cells compactly.
- Rewrite source-language sentence structure into clear, idiomatic English. Avoid translationese, literal word order, and source-language clause stacking, while preserving the original meaning precisely.
- Prefer active voice when the actor is known or recoverable from context. Use passive voice only when the actor is unknown, intentionally backgrounded, or when passive voice is the more natural English choice.
- Restore omitted Korean subjects or objects when English requires them, but avoid overusing vague subjects such as 'it', 'this', or 'there' when a clear noun phrase would be more natural.
- Do not mechanically translate discourse connectors. Avoid excessive sentence-initial 'And', 'But', 'So', 'Meanwhile', or 'In conclusion'; express the relationship through natural English sentence structure when possible.
- Avoid heavy nominalization when a direct English verb phrase is clearer. Prefer natural verbs such as decide, review, approve, manage, or report over unnecessary noun phrases when the source meaning allows it.
- Use English articles and singular/plural forms consistently. Add a, an, the, or plural markers when English grammar requires them, without inventing specificity that is not supported by the source.
- For headings and slide titles, use natural English heading capitalization. Prefer Title Case for major headings and slide titles, and Sentence case for body labels, table cells, field labels, and inline UI text unless a purpose-specific rule says otherwise.
- When no specific purpose is selected, translate into natural English while preserving the source style and document type.
- For source-language term preservation, keep proper nouns, brand names, product names, system names, company names, acronyms, code identifiers, API names, model names, and official named entities in the source language when translating them would be ambiguous or misleading.
- Do not preserve ordinary source-language role names, permission names, task names, feature descriptions, table labels, or operational phrases merely because they look like business terms. Translate them into compact, natural English.
- Translate the surrounding sentence naturally into English. Preserve a source term only when it is a named entity, acronym, identifier, or explicitly supplied terminology item.

Final output rules:
- JSON array only.
- No prose. No markdown. No reasoning. No <think>.
- All ids preserved.
- Do not summarize the context.
- Do not omit content from SOURCE_TEXT.

NEXT: 그런데 뜻밖에 이처럼 성황을 이루어서 장소가 매우 협착한 까닭에, 여러분끼리 서로간 친하는 기회를 드리려는 다과회가 무슨 강연회처럼 되었습니다."", "source": ""에― 아직 개학을 아니 헌 학교도 있어서 미처 올라오지 못한 대원이 많을 줄 알었습니다."}, {"id": 8, "context": "PREVIOUS: "에― 아직 개학을 아니 헌 학교도 있어서 미처 올라오지 못한 대원이 많을 줄 알었습니다.
NEXT: 하고 일장의 인사를 베푼 뒤에 으흠으흠 하고 헛기침을 해서 목소리를 가다듬더니,", "source": "그런데 뜻밖에 이처럼 성황을 이루어서 장소가 매우 협착한 까닭에, 여러분끼리 서로간 친하는 기회를 드리려는 다과회가 무슨 강연회처럼 되었습니다.""}, {"id": 9, "context": "PREVIOUS: 그런데 뜻밖에 이처럼 성황을 이루어서 장소가 매우 협착한 까닭에, 여러분끼리 서로간 친하는 기회를 드리려는 다과회가 무슨 강연회처럼 되었습니다."
NEXT: "금년에는 여러 가지로 지장이 많았는데도 불구하고 작년보다도 거진 곱절이나 되는 놀라울 만한 성적을 보게 됐습니다. 이것은 오직 동족을 사랑하는 여러분의 열성과, 문맹을 한 사람이라도 더 물리치려는 헌신적 노력의 결과인 것이 물론입니다. 그러므로 주 최자측으로서 여러분의 수고를 감사할 뿐 아니라, 우리 계몽운동의 장래를 위해서 경축하기를 마지않는 바입니다."", "source": "하고 일장의 인사를 베푼 뒤에 으흠으흠 하고 헛기침을 해서 목소리를 가다듬더니,"}, {"id": 10, "context": "PREVIOUS: 하고 일장의 인사를 베푼 뒤에 으흠으흠 하고 헛기침을 해서 목소리를 가다듬더니,
NEXT: 처음에는 늦게 들어오는 사람들 때문에 수성수성하던 장내가 인제는 기침 소리 하나 없이 조용해졌다. 사회자는 말을 이어,", "source": ""금년에는 여러 가지로 지장이 많았는데도 불구하고 작년보다도 거진 곱절이나 되는 놀라울 만한 성적을 보게 됐습니다. 이것은 오직 동족을 사랑하는 여러분의 열성과, 문맹을 한 사람이라도 더 물리치려는 헌신적 노력의 결과인 것이 물론입니다. 그러므로 주 최자측으로서 여러분의 수고를 감사할 뿐 아니라, 우리 계몽운동의 장래를 위해서 경축하기를 마지않는 바입니다.""}, {"id": 11, "context": "PREVIOUS: "금년에는 여러 가지로 지장이 많았는데도 불구하고 작년보다도 거진 곱절이나 되는 놀라울 만한 성적을 보게 됐습니다. 이것은 오직 동족을 사랑하는 여러분의 열성과, 문맹을 한 사람이라도 더 물리치려는 헌신적 노력의 결과인 것이 물론입니다. 그러므로 주 최자측으로서 여러분의 수고를 감사할 뿐 아니라, 우리 계몽운동의 장래를 위해서 경축하기를 마지않는 바입니다."
NEXT: "긴 말씀은 허지 않겠으나, 차나 마셔 가면서 간담적으로 피차에 의견도 교환하고, 그 동안에 분투한 체험담도 들려 주셔서 앞으로 이 운동을 계속하는 데 크게 참고가 되게 해주시 기를 바라는 바입니다."", "source": "처음에는 늦게 들어오는 사람들 때문에 수성수성하던 장내가 인제는 기침 소리 하나 없이 조용해졌다. 사회자는 말을 이어,"}]
